title: Gujarati Sev Tameta Nu Shaak

subs.name: omit for a no-onion version; add an extra pinch of asafoetida

ingredients.name: jaggery or sugar
visual_checks: balances the tomato acidity — essential in Gujarati cooking
ingredients.notes: balances the tomato acidity — essential in Gujarati cooking

subs.name: 1 tsp sugar or a small piece of dates blended with a little water

ingredients.name: sev (thin fried chickpea noodles), store-bought
visual_checks: nylon sev (the thinnest variety) is traditional; any sev or bhujia works
ingredients.notes: nylon sev (the thinnest variety) is traditional; any sev or bhujia works

subs.name: crushed bhujia mix or thin sev namkeen — different spice profile but similar crunch

steps.text: Heat oil in a kadai on medium-high. Add mustard seeds and wait until they pop. Add cumin seeds, asafoetida and curry leaves; they will crackle in about 10 seconds. Add the green chillies and onion and sauté until the onion is translucent and soft, about 4–5 minutes.

Stir in the ginger and garlic and cook for 30 seconds until the raw smell goes. Add turmeric, red chilli powder and coriander powder; stir for 20 seconds then immediately add the chopped tomatoes and a pinch of salt to prevent burning.

Cook the tomatoes on medium flame, breaking them up with the back of a spoon, until they fully collapse and the oil begins to separate from the sides of the masala — about 8 minutes. The gravy should be thick and jammy, not watery.

Add jaggery and salt, stir to combine, then add 0.25 cup water to loosen the gravy to a pourable consistency. Taste and adjust sweet-sour-spice balance — Gujarati shaak leans sweet. Simmer for 2 more minutes.

Take the kadai off the flame. Scatter sev generously over the surface of the gravy — do not stir it in. Top with coriander leaves and serve immediately with rotis, thepla or phulkas. Once the sev soaks up gravy it loses its crunch, so serve and eat without delay.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: jaggery → 1 tsp sugar or a small piece of dates blended with a little water; besan → crushed bhujia mix or thin sev namkeen — different spice profile but similar crunch; onion → omit for a no-onion version; add an extra pinch of asafoetida
